items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 26 at 6:13AM CDT by NWS Aberdeen SD
items.summary:
Through 9 AM CDT, a couple areas of light rain, mainly north of
U.S. Highway 12 and east of U.S. Highway 281, are moving across
northeast South Dakota into west central Minnesota. At the same
time, temperatures are also cooling through the 30s, on their way
to the freezing mark or colder. Over the next few hours, road
temperatures may remain warm enough to prevent this rain from
freezing on contact with the ground, despite air temperature
dropping to and below 32F degrees. Nevertheless, morning
items.area: Traverse; Big Stone; Brown; Marshall; Roberts; Day

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 26 at 6:44AM EDT by NWS Melbourne FL
items.summary:
Satellite imagery, traffic cameras, and surface observations
indicate patchy dense fog is occurring or may develop across
portions of Lake and Volusia counties. Reduced visibilities of
one mile or less at times is possible through 9 AM.

Motorists should be alert for sudden changes in visibility. When
encountering fog, slow down, increase following distance, and use
low-beam headlights.
items.area: Inland Volusia; Northern Lake County; Coastal Volusia; Southern Lake County

items.title: Small Craft Advisory issued March 26 at 3:32AM PDT until March 27 at 3:00AM PDT by NWS Los Angeles/Oxnard CA
items.summary:
* WHAT...Northwest winds 20 to 30 kt with gusts up to 35 kt and
combined seas 10 to 12 ft when conditions are worst.

* WHERE...Point Piedras Blancas to Point Sal from 10 to 60 NM.

* WHEN...For the Gale Warning, until 6 AM PDT early this
morning. For the Small Craft Advisory, until 3 AM PDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Strong winds will create dangerous sea conditions
which could capsize or damage small and large vessels.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...See the Coastal Waters Forecast (CWFLOX)
for more.
items.area: Point Piedras Blancas to Point Sal from 10 to 60 NM

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 26 at 6:17AM EDT by NWS Gaylord MI
items.summary:
Spotty light precipitation is developing over eastern upper and
far northern lower Michigan early this morning. This is falling as
a mix of freezing rain, sleet, rain, and perhaps snow. Some roads
are likely to become icy in parts of eastern upper and far
northern lower Michigan early this morning.

Mixed precipitation is expected to diminish and end over eastern
upper Michigan this morning. Over far northern lower Michigan,
warming temperatures will change precipitation to plain rain by
late mo
items.area: Emmet; Cheboygan; Presque Isle; Western Chippewa; Central Chippewa; Southeast Chippewa; Western Mackinac; Eastern Mackinac; Mackinac Island/Bois Blanc Island; Beaver Island and surrounding islands; Charlevoix

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 26 at 5:57AM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
items.summary:
Areas of fog are expected, with patches of dense fog as well producing
visibilities around 1/4 mile in a few locations. Visibilities will
improve by the mid morning hours.

Motorists should be alert for sudden changes in visibility. When
encountering fog, slow down, increase following distance, and use
low-beam headlights.
items.area: Suwannee; Baker; Union; Bradford; Gilchrist; Eastern Hamilton; Eastern Alachua; Western Hamilton; Western Clay; Western Alachua; Western Putnam; Northern Columbia; Southeastern Columbia; Southwestern Columbia; Echols

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 26 at 1:31AM AKDT by NWS Juneau AK
items.summary:
A series of low pressure systems will approach the southern
panhandle through the end of the work week and into the weekend,
with multiple fronts/troughs lifting north through the region.
Multiple rounds of snow showers or rain/snow mix are expected for
Annette Island, Ketchikan, Prince of Wales Island and Port
Alexander. Heavier showers could reduce visibility down to 1/2
mile or less, with snowfall rates as high as 1 inch per hour. One
to three inches of snow is possible during the nighttime a
items.area: Western Kupreanof and Kuiu Island; Ketchikan Gateway Borough; Annette Island

items.title: Winter Weather Advisory issued March 26 at 1:21AM AKDT until March 26 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Juneau AK
items.summary:
* WHAT...Snow. Additional snow accumulations of 3 to 7 inches.

* WHERE...Prince of Wales Island.

* WHEN...Until 10 PM AKDT Thursday evening.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Several waves of precipitation expected
with one wave early Thursday morning, and another midday
Thursday. Highest accumulations will be on the south side of the
island and on the inland higher elevations of the road system.
Afternoon temperatures near the coasts could reach the mid to
upper
items.area: Prince of Wales Island

items.title: Winter Weather Advisory issued March 26 at 3:00AM MDT until March 26 at 9:00AM MDT by NWS Great Falls MT
items.summary:
* WHAT...Snow expected. Total snow accumulations 2 to 4 inches, with
a few isolated areas near 6 inches.

* WHERE...The Highwood, Little Belt, Judith, and Snowy Mountains,
Judith Basin County and Judith Gap, and Fergus County below 4500ft.

* WHEN...Until 9 AM MDT this morning.

* IMPACTS...Roads, and especially bridges and overpasses, will
likely become slick and hazardous.
items.area: Judith Basin County and Judith Gap; Fergus County below 4500ft; Little Belt and Highwood Mountains; Snowy and Judith Mountains

items.title: Wind Advisory issued March 26 at 3:36AM CDT until March 27 at 7:00PM CDT by NWS Amarillo TX
items.summary:
* WHAT...Temperatures at or above 105 degrees expected.

* WHERE...Palo Duro Canyon.

* WHEN...For the Heat Advisory, from noon today to 8 PM CDT this
evening. For the Wind Advisory, from 1 AM to 7 PM CDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result. Hot
temperatures may cause heat illnesses.
items.area: Palo Duro Canyon

items.area: Central and Southeast Phillips; Central and Southern Valley; Petroleum; Garfield; McCone; Southwest Phillips

items.title: Marine Weather Statement issued March 26 at 2:58AM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
items.summary:
Moist air and light winds will produce areas of fog early this morning
across the nearshore coastal waters and inland waterways. These
conditions may produce some dense fog with visibility possibly
below 1 NM.

Marine interests should exercise caution due to dense fog. Visibilities
may be restricted to less than 1 nm at times. Navigation may be
hazardous in restricted waterways and channels.
items.area: Coastal waters from Altamaha Sound to Fernandina Beach FL out 20 NM; Coastal waters from Fernandina Beach to St. Augustine FL out 20 NM
